notes.roots::root-ca-certificate-requirements-: Same as 1-8.1: Go's x509 never emits subjectUniqueID.
Allowed RSA sizes (2048, 4096) are multiples of 8. goodkey/good_key.go:333-346.
Exponent hardcoded to 65537 (cmd/ceremony/rsa.go:15) and GoodKey requires E==65537 (goodkey/good_key.go:308-310).
Same as 3.15: pairing is YAML-driven; LE's deployed ceremonies use ECDSAWithSHA384 with P-384 (test/certs/root-ceremony-ecdsa.yaml:9,14).
cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286-296 sets BasicConstraintsValid=true and IsCA=true, causing Go's x509 to emit the basicConstraints extension.
Not applicable: ceremony never emits SCT extensions on roots.
verifyProfile rejects empty country (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:138-140), so all roots get a countryName attribute.
Curve25519/Curve448 are not in stringToCurve (cmd/ceremony/ecdsa.go:15-19) and would fail keyGenConfig.validate's allowedCurves check (cmd/ceremony/main.go:103-107, 122-124).
Roots cannot have policies (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:142-146); constraint vacuously satisfied.

Root profile validation does not require CRLURL (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:148-154 only requires it for intermediate/crossCert). LE root YAMLs (test/certs/root-ceremony-rsa.yaml, root-ceremony-ecdsa.yaml) omit `crl-url` so cmd/ceremony/cert.go:211-214 leaves CRLDistributionPoints empty and the extension is not emitted.

Go's crypto/x509 produces valid DER; zlint's e_old_root_ca_rsa_mod_less_than_2048_bits and other parsing-based lints would reject malformed DER. Both pre-issuance lint (cmd/ceremony/main.go:49-64) and post-issuance lint (main.go:70-95) re-parse and validate.
Same as 3.4: SHA-1 RSA signatures are not selectable from AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78).
Not applicable: AIA is not emitted on roots.
Go's x509.CreateCertificate emits AKI with only keyIdentifier when AuthorityKeyId is set; it does not emit authorityCertIssuer.

For root certs MaxPathLenZero is NOT set (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:328-337 only sets it for intermediate/crossCert/requestCert), so pathLenConstraint is absent. Comment at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:244-251 explicitly documents this.
Go's x509.SHA512WithRSA produces the prescribed DER. AllowedSigAlgs includes SHA512WithRSA (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:74).

LE roots are TLS server auth only; X1 and X2 were both included in Mozilla's store prior to 2025-03-15 anyway, and Boulder issues server-auth-only intermediates (the 'server' EKU default at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-258).
Roots are explicitly rejected if any EKU is configured: cmd/ceremony/cert.go:243-251 (`root certificates MUST NOT have an EKU extension`).
Go's x509.CreateCertificate emits AKI with only keyIdentifier; it does not emit authorityCertSerialNumber.

Go's x509.CreateCertificate encodes the same AlgorithmIdentifier in tbsCertificate.signature and outer signatureAlgorithm; zlint enforces e_signature_algorithm_not_supported / e_inner_outer_signature_algorithm_mismatch.
Go's pkix.Name encoder emits one ATV per RDN. zlint enforces.
RSASSA-PSS not in AllowedSigAlgs; vacuously satisfied.
Same as 1-5.1: notBefore comes from YAML, no code-level guard against future notBefore.

Boulder caps root validity at <25 years (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:313-317) and lint_root_ca_cert_validity_period_greater_than_25_years.go enforces the upper bound, but there is no minimum-8-year (2922 day) enforcement in code. Operationally LE roots are minted with >=20-year lifetimes (X1 2015-06-04..2035-06-04; X2 2020-09-04..2040-09-15), satisfying the floor.

16 random bytes (128 bits) sourced from HSM RNG via hsmRandReader (cmd/ceremony/key.go:23-30 calling pkcs11 GenerateRandom), satisfying the 64-bit CSPRNG output requirement. cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225-229.

In rootCeremony (cmd/ceremony/main.go:587-595), makeTemplate is called with tbcs=nil and signAndWriteCert is invoked with template as both tbs and issuer, making issuer DN byte-identical to subject DN.
RSASSA-PSS not in AllowedSigAlgs; vacuously satisfied.
AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78) restricts to SHA-256/384/512 with RSA or ECDSA (no PSS), all permitted BR §7.1.3.2 encodings. zlint enforces specific DER encodings.
curveToOIDDER for P-521 is `{6, 5, 43, 129, 4, 0, 35}` (cmd/ceremony/ecdsa.go:25) producing the prescribed encoding.
Go's x509.CreateCertificate emits subjectKeyIdentifier as non-critical.
Go's x509.SHA384WithRSA produces the prescribed DER. AllowedSigAlgs includes SHA384WithRSA (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:73).
MD5 is not in AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78); unsupported algorithm strings are rejected at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:299-302.
Boulder ceremony exposes no optional state/locality/postal/street fields (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:24-68); they are never emitted in roots.
SKID is a deterministic SHA-256 truncation of the SPKI bit string (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:200-201); two distinct keys virtually never collide. RFC 5280 §4.2.1.2 method (1) variant from RFC 7093 §2.

Operational: subsequent reissuances must use byte-identical YAML strings to produce the same DN encoding. Not checked in code at root-ceremony time, but the cross-cert path explicitly checks `bytes.Equal(toBeCrossSigned.RawSubject, lintCert.RawSubject)` (cmd/ceremony/main.go:707-709) when cross-signing.
Go's x509.SHA256WithRSA produces the prescribed DER. AllowedSigAlgs includes SHA256WithRSA (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:72).

Root profile validation does not configure issuer-url (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:148-154 only requires it for intermediate/crossCert). LE root YAMLs omit issuer-url so cmd/ceremony/cert.go:215-218 leaves IssuingCertificateURL empty; AIA is not emitted on roots.
verifyProfile rejects empty organization (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:135-137).
AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78) limits to SHA256/384/512-WithRSA and ECDSA-WithSHA256/384/512; PSS is not exposed.

Subject values come from YAML (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:30-34); code does not pin them to ISRG/US. Operationally LE ceremonies use C=US, O=Internet Security Research Group, CN=ISRG Root XN as confirmed by deployed roots.
keyGenConfig.validate restricts RSA modulus to exactly 2048 or 4096 (cmd/ceremony/main.go:116-118); goodKeyRSA further enforces a whitelist of 2048/3072/4096 (goodkey/good_key.go:333-346).

The ceremony template only sets the BR-defined extensions (BasicConstraints, KeyUsage, SubjectKeyId; AKI implicitly for self-signed) and never sets ExtraExtensions for root ceremonies (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286-296).
Not applicable: AIA is not emitted on roots; constraint vacuously satisfied.

Serial is generated from 16 random bytes via the HSM RNG (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225-229) then converted with big.NewInt(0).SetBytes(serial); MSB is zero-padded by the encoder so the serial is positive and non-zero with overwhelming probability. zlint's e_invalid_serial_number_size and e_serial_number_must_be_positive also run.
Subject() (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96) provides no DNS/IP/Email SANs and the template at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286-296 doesn't set DNSNames/IPAddresses/etc. SAN is never emitted on roots.
Serial is freshly random for each ceremony (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225-229); roots are infrequent so non-sequentiality is trivially satisfied by CSPRNG output.
Not applicable: ceremony never emits nameConstraints (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286-296).
Go's x509.MarshalPKIXPublicKey (cmd/ceremony/key.go:71-74) produces the canonical AlgorithmIdentifier with explicit NULL parameters for RSA keys. zlint enforces the specific DER encoding.

SHA-1 is not in AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78); SHA-1 signatures cannot be produced by the ceremony tool. The narrow same-key reissuance exception is not implemented, but LE has no plan to use it.
Not applicable: ceremony never emits nameConstraints.
Pairing of signature algorithm to key curve is YAML-driven (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:27, 71-78). Code does not enforce that P-256 keys use ECDSAWithSHA256. zlint enforces the matching at lint time.
Go's x509.CreateCertificate emits identical AlgorithmIdentifier DER in tbsCertificate.signature and outer signatureAlgorithm; zlint enforces e_inner_outer_signature_algorithm_mismatch.
curveToOIDDER for P-384 is `{6, 5, 43, 129, 4, 0, 34}` (cmd/ceremony/ecdsa.go:24) producing the prescribed encoding.
The ceremony tool does not emit SCT extensions on roots; roots are not logged to CT. Not applicable.
verifyProfile rejects any policies on root certs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:142-146: `policies should not be set on root certs`).
EdDSA cannot be generated: keyGenConfig only allows 'rsa' or 'ecdsa' (cmd/ceremony/main.go:113-115) and GoodKey rejects non-RSA/ECDSA types (goodkey/good_key.go:144-150).

LE roots do not sign OCSP responses, so Digital Signature is intentionally omitted. The YAMLs skip the n_ca_digital_signature_not_set lint (test/certs/root-ceremony-rsa.yaml:23-25, root-ceremony-ecdsa.yaml:23-25).
AllowedSigAlgs map (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78) lists only SHA-256/384/512 hashes.
Roots cannot have policies (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:142-146); vacuously satisfied.
Only C/O/CN are configured (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:24-68) and Go's pkix.Name encodes RDNs in the canonical order. zlint enforces RDN ordering.

Country is a free-form YAML string (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:34); code only enforces non-empty. LE roots use 'US' which is a valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code. zlint's e_subject_country_not_iso would catch invalid codes.
Operational/external: enforced by Chrome's distrust decision based on key-generation date recorded in CCADB, not by Boulder. ISRG Root X1 key generated 2015-06; X2 in 2020-09.
Roots cannot have policies (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:142-146); vacuously satisfied.
N/A: LE only operates TLS roots; no Microsoft Code-Signing or Time-Stamping roots.
KeyUsages must be non-empty (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:239-241 rejects ku==0). YAML configures the bits at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:59.
Operational/external: Mozilla policy-driven trust-bit removal based on CCADB-tracked key-gen date. Not enforced by Boulder.
Go's pkix.Name encoder emits one ATV per RDN by default. Enforced by zlint's e_subject_dn_country_not_printable_string and related RDN-structure lints.
RSASSA-PSS not in AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78); PSS-SHA256 signatures cannot be produced. The constraint is vacuously satisfied.
The ceremony template does not set PermittedDNSDomains/ExcludedDNSDomains/etc. for any cert type (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286-296), so nameConstraints is never emitted.

goodKeyECDSA (goodkey/good_key.go:174-260) implements NIST SP800-56A §5.6.2.3.2 full public key validation (point-at-infinity check, range check, on-curve check, order check). GoodKey is invoked on the freshly-generated root key in postIssuanceLinting (cmd/ceremony/main.go:89).
Like 4-3.3: YAML-configured. LE root YAMLs include 'CRL Sign'. zlint enforces e_ca_keyusage_missing_crlsign.
Operational/external: enforced at CCADB inclusion-request time, not by Boulder code.

Boulder does not require Cert Sign to be set; the bits are YAML-configured via stringToKeyUsage (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:181-185). LE root YAMLs (test/certs/root-ceremony-rsa.yaml:20-22, root-ceremony-ecdsa.yaml:20-22) include 'Cert Sign'. zlint enforces e_ca_keyusage_missing_keycertsign for CAs.
N/A to LE roots: ISRG Root X1 (2015) and X2 (2020) keys are outside this date range.

The certProfile struct exposes only Country, Organization, CommonName YAML fields (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:24-68); there is no way to configure an OU. Subject() builds a pkix.Name with no OrganizationalUnit (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96).
rsaArgs (cmd/ceremony/rsa.go:22-53) uses CKM_RSA_PKCS_KEY_PAIR_GEN producing rsaEncryption OID keys; PSS keys are not generated.

16-byte random serial = 128 bits, capped at 159 bits per BR. cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225 uses 16 bytes (128 bits) which is well below 2^159. zlint enforces n_subject_serial_number_more_than_twenty_octets.
Operational: requires distinct Subject DN and fresh key per Microsoft root addition. Not code-enforced; each LE root ceremony does generate a fresh HSM key (cmd/ceremony/key.go:48-69).

LE's PKI hierarchy is dedicated to TLS server auth. Ceremony defaults intermediates to id-kp-serverAuth only (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-258) and rejects EKUs other than 'none'/'server'/'both' (with 'both' being phased out by 2026-06-15 per cmd/ceremony/cert.go:259-280).

keyGenConfig.validate restricts to 'rsa' or 'ecdsa' (cmd/ceremony/main.go:113-115); generateKey calls rsaGenerate or ecGenerate (cmd/ceremony/key.go:58-69). GoodKey (goodkey/good_key.go:144-150) rejects anything other than *rsa.PublicKey or *ecdsa.PublicKey.
N/A to LE roots: ISRG keys generated in 2015 and 2020, not 2008-2009.
Only the standard CABF-defined extensions are emitted; all are in the public-Internet context.
The 25-year cap in cmd/ceremony/cert.go:313 and the CPCPS lint apply to any root cert produced by the ceremony tool, including reissuances.

Boulder's online CA never signs subscriber certs with a root key; subscribers are signed by intermediates (ca/ca.go and the WFE/RA design). The ceremony tool's root path also doesn't produce subscriber certs.
curveToOIDDER for P-256 is `{6, 8, 42, 134, 72, 206, 61, 3, 1, 7}` (cmd/ceremony/ecdsa.go:23) which yields the prescribed AlgorithmIdentifier when wrapped by SPKI.
Go's x509.CreateCertificate marks keyUsage critical when KeyUsage != 0.
For self-signed roots Go's x509 sets AKI keyIdentifier = SKID. SKID computed at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:220-223.
N/A to LE roots: ISRG keys generated in 2015 and 2020, not 2010-2011.

Root cert template does not set AuthorityKeyId (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286-296; only intermediate/crossCert ceremonies do at main.go:635, 691). Go's encoder produces a self-AKI implicitly only if SubjectKeyId is also set; in self-signed certs Go emits AKI=SKID and marks it non-critical.
verifyProfile rejects empty common-name (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:132-134).
Go's crypto/x509 emits PKCS1v15 signatures with explicit NULL parameters. zlint enforces the specific DER encoding.
Go's crypto/x509 emits ECDSA signature AlgorithmIdentifiers without parameters; zlint enforces.

Go's crypto/x509 always produces v3 certificates via x509.CreateCertificate, used in signAndWriteCert (cmd/ceremony/main.go:508). Enforced universally by zlint (e.g., e_old_root_ca_rsa_mod_less_than_2048_bits and version lints in the global registry pulled in at linter/linter.go:217-232).
ecArgs supplies CKA_EC_PARAMS as the DER OID of the named curve (cmd/ceremony/ecdsa.go:22-26, 43), producing a namedCurve encoding. Go's x509 marshals ECDSA SPKI with namedCurve.
RSASSA-PSS not in AllowedSigAlgs; vacuously satisfied.
Go's pkix.Name encodes Organization as UTF8String or PrintableString; zlint enforces 64-char limit. ISRG roots use 'Internet Security Research Group' (~32 chars).
Go's pkix.Name marshals to a Name CHOICE -> RDNSequence; zlint enforces.
Pairing of P-521 with SHA-512 is YAML-driven; LE does not currently use P-521 for roots.
Subject DN is built via pkix.Name in cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96; Go's x509 encodes attributes per RFC/CABF rules and the zlint registry enforces the full BR §7.1.4 subject encoding ruleset.
signAndWriteCert calls cert.CheckSignatureFrom(issuer) after self-signing (cmd/ceremony/main.go:518-527), verifying the signature against the cert's own public key for the root case.
stringToKeyUsage (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:181-185) only exposes Digital Signature, CRL Sign, and Cert Sign; the other bits cannot be set via YAML.
Go's crypto/x509 produces DER-conformant encodings for all standard extensions; zlint validates DER.
Roots cannot have policies (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:142-146); vacuously satisfied.
RSASSA-PSS not in AllowedSigAlgs; vacuously satisfied.
Not applicable: ceremony never emits nameConstraints.
Go's pkix.Name encodes single-char Country values as PrintableString. zlint enforces length and encoding constraints; LE uses 'US' (2 chars).
Public exponent is hardcoded to 65537 in cmd/ceremony/rsa.go:14-16, verified by rsaPub at cmd/ceremony/rsa.go:64-66, and rechecked by GoodKey at goodkey/good_key.go:308-310 (rejects E != 65537).
subjectKeyID is computed via generateSKID (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:187-202, 220-223) using RFC 7093 method (SHA-256 truncated to 160 bits) and is always set on the template.
Roots cannot have policies (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:142-146); vacuously satisfied.
Enforced in code at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:312-317 (`validity >= 9132*24*time.Hour`) and by lint linter/lints/cpcps/lint_root_ca_cert_validity_period_greater_than_25_years.go.
notBefore is taken verbatim from the ceremony YAML (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:304-307). Code does not check it against time-of-signing; this is an operational/ceremony-script constraint.
65537 = 2^16 + 1, within the SHOULD range. cmd/ceremony/rsa.go:15.
Go encodes CN as UTF8String/PrintableString; zlint enforces 64-char cap. LE roots use 'ISRG Root X1' / 'ISRG Root X2'.
cmd/ceremony/cert.go:289 sets `IsCA: true`.

stringToCurve only contains P-256/P-384/P-521 (cmd/ceremony/ecdsa.go:15-19); allowedCurves enforces the same (cmd/ceremony/main.go:103-107). goodKeyECDSA's goodCurve whitelist matches (goodkey/good_key.go:271-284).
N/A: LE does not issue S/MIME certs.
Roots cannot have policies (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:142-146); vacuously satisfied.

Roots are self-signed; the issuer-DN+serial uniqueness only matters per-issuing-CA. CSPRNG random 128-bit serials make collision astronomically unlikely. Operationally each root ceremony produces one cert.
Go's x509.CreateCertificate marks basicConstraints critical when BasicConstraintsValid=true (set at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:288).
N/A to LE roots: ISRG keys generated in 2015 and 2020, not 2012-2014.
Go's x509.CreateCertificate never emits issuerUniqueID/subjectUniqueID; not exposed by ceremony YAML. zlint also enforces this.
Only one of each attribute is configurable in the YAML (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:30-34); zlint enforces uniqueness.

keyGenConfig allows RSA 2048/4096 and ECDSA P-256/P-384/P-521 (cmd/ceremony/main.go:116-124); code does not specifically pin to RSA-4096 or P-384. Operationally LE root ceremonies have used RSA-4096 (X1) and P-384 (X2) as committed via ceremony YAML.
notes.cross-certs::cross-certified-subordinate: IsCA=true at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:289.
goodkey allows only modulus bitlen exactly 2048/3072/4096 (all multiples of 8) (goodkey/good_key.go:337-342).
Procedural: ceremony YAML reflects the EKUs the operator believes the subordinate is authorized to assert. No code-level authorization check.
Ceremony does not embed SCTs in cross-certs; not applicable.

The cross-cert's CRL is generated by the same ceremony tool's `crl` subcommand (cmd/ceremony/crl.go) which adds a critical onlyContainsCACerts IDP via crl/idp/idp.go:62-79 -- matching CCADB §6.2 / Mozilla §6.1.2 partitioning. See lint linter/lints/cpcps/lint_crl_has_idp.go.
cmd/ceremony/cert.go:336 sets cert.SubjectKeyId = tbcs.SubjectKeyId for crossCert. Go emits it.
No nameConstraints emitted.

Ceremony CRL adds critical IDP with onlyContainsCACerts (cmd/ceremony/crl.go:39-43 -> crl/idp/idp.go:62-79). Shard CRLs from issuance/crl.go:103-105 emit IDP with fullName matching the CRLDP URL. linter/lints/cpcps/lint_crl_has_idp.go enforces.
Go emits AIA non-critical.

ISRG Root X1 (added 2016) and X2 (added 2021) predate 2025-03-15 so the cascade does not apply to current LE crosses. For any future post-2025-03-15 TLS root LE adds, ceremony emits exactly [serverAuth] or [serverAuth,clientAuth] (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280).
Subject is byte-identical to the existing CA cert (cmd/ceremony/main.go:707-709); Go's pkix.Name encoder produces RDN sequences with single AVAs.
Go stdlib emits AKI non-critical; zlint cabf_br covers this.
LE does not issue technically-constrained TLS sub-CAs; ceremony has no support for emitting nameConstraints.
verifyProfile rejects any policy set other than exactly [2.23.140.1.2.1] (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:158-161) so anyPolicy is impossible to set even for affiliated cross-certs.
Cross-certs are not CT-pre-certified in LE's flow; AllowedSigAlgs excludes SHA-1 regardless (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78).
Limited EKU palette in cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280 means only EKUs with a clear reason can appear.
Cross-cert copies the existing cert's SKI (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:336), which is already RFC 5280-compliant. Uniqueness for the same pubkey is preserved by reusing the identical SKI.
cmd/ceremony/main.go:713-715 explicitly checks `lintCert.NotBefore.Before(toBeCrossSigned.NotBefore)` and aborts. The one-day-prior-to-signing branch is enforced procedurally via the YAML config.
Cross-key-reuse across EKUs: not enforced in Boulder code. Operationally LE does not mix purposes on the same key.
Only one PolicyInformation is present so it is trivially first (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:158-161).
SHA-1 is not in AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78); the narrow §7.1.3.2.1 exception is not used by LE.

Cross-key-reuse across EKUs: not enforced by Boulder code. LE operationally only ever creates cross-certs that mirror the existing intermediate's EKU set, so the constraint holds by construction; this would need cross-cert inventory review to confirm.
goodkey accepts only *rsa.PublicKey from x509.ParsePKIXPublicKey, which decodes only rsaEncryption (not id-RSASSA-PSS) (cmd/ceremony/main.go:551, 555).
Same-key-as-root exemption: not exercised by LE's current cross-cert program (LE-issued cross-certs are over intermediates, not over a root sharing SPKI).
x509.CreateRevocationList (cmd/ceremony/crl.go:55, issuance/crl.go:116) emits v2 CRLs; zlint enforces.

Ceremony constructs Subject from profile fields (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96) and then enforces RawSubject byte-equality between toBeCrossSigned and the freshly built lintCert at cmd/ceremony/main.go:707-709. A mismatch aborts issuance.
Ceremony only emits [serverAuth] or [serverAuth, clientAuth] for cross-certs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280); never combines serverAuth with emailProtection, codeSigning, or timeStamping.
Same as 2.8.1 for subjectUniqueID.
If 'Digital Signature' is in profile YAML KeyUsages (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:181-185), it is set. LE does not delegate OCSP signing from cross-certs.
Ceremony does not embed SCTs in cross-certs; not applicable.
Ceremony's certProfile only models the listed extensions (AKI, BC, KU, SKI, EKU, certPolicies, CRLDP, AIA); no escape hatch for arbitrary extensions (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:19-67).

Procedural pre-condition outside Boulder code: the issuing CA MUST confirm the existing CA cert was BR-compliant at issuance. Boulder/ceremony enforces no such check; for LE the existing certs are LE-issued roots/intermediates whose BR compliance is independently audited, but the confirmation lives outside the source tree.
Ceremony forbids additional OIDs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:158-161 requires exactly one policy); LE CP/CPS documents the DV OID.

Cross-certs whose subject pubkey corresponds to a TLS-dedicated root: ceremony emits exactly [serverAuth] when profile.EKUs='server' (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:254-258); for crosses of intermediates with [serverAuth] existing EKU, the EKU-equality check (cert.go:282-284) ensures match. After 2026-06-15 only 'server' is allowed (cert.go:269-277).
Mozilla's SHA-1 ban: not relevant since AllowedSigAlgs excludes SHA-1 (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78).

Uniqueness of (issuer, serial) across all cross-certs ever issued by the issuer is not enforced by code; relies on 128-bit random serial space (collision probability negligible) plus operational ceremony records. No DB check exists because issuance is offline.
No nameConstraints emitted; not applicable.
Go emits CRLDP non-critical.
verifyProfile requires CRLURL for crossCert (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:148-151); set on template (cert.go:212-214) so Go emits CRLDP.

Ceremony always emits an EKU on crossCert (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:243-280 sets at least serverAuth for non-root). The 'shares-its-private-key-with-root' exemption is not relied upon for LE's current cross-cert program (the long-expired DST<->ISRG Root X1 cross would have qualified).
goodkey allows only RSA 2048/3072/4096 (goodkey/good_key.go:333-346); <2048 is rejected.
Go stdlib emits DER-encoded extensions per their ASN.1 definitions; zlint enforces.
Same as 4.4.3 -- 'CRL Sign' must be in profile YAML KeyUsages list. Zlint cabf_br enforces.
CCADB-disclosure-URL match is a procedural requirement; profile.CRLURL is set in the ceremony YAML and must match the CCADB record for the issuer. Not validated by Boulder code.
x509.CreateCertificate emits identical AlgorithmIdentifier in both fields (Go stdlib); zlint catches divergence.
Same as 4.6.21 -- pre-2025-06-15 disclosure permitted 'both'; LE's E1/E2/R3/R10/R11 cross-certs reflect this.

ISRG roots predate 2024-04-15 in Apple's program (X1 2016, X2 2021); for future post-2024-04-15 applicant roots ceremony emits at most [serverAuth, clientAuth] (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280) so post-2024-04-15 applicants would comply.
Go's x509.CreateCertificate sets tbs.signature and outer signatureAlgorithm from the same SignatureAlgorithm field; zlint catches divergence.

Chrome's 3-year SHOULD is not enforced in code; ceremony allows up to 8 years (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:318-323). LE's outstanding 5-year cross-certs exceed Chrome's SHOULD by design; non-blocking SHOULD.
Go's CRLDistributionPoints emitter uses fullName with no reasons/cRLIssuer when given simple URLs (template.CRLDistributionPoints).
Only one caIssuers URL emitted (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:216-218); ordering moot.
Single URL from profile.CRLURL is by definition the first/only GeneralName (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:212-214).

All LE cross-certs use the restricted form; profile.EKUs of 'server' or 'both' (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280) cannot include anyExtendedKeyUsage. The organization-mismatch check at cmd/ceremony/main.go:736-739 also forbids unrestricted cross-signs to other organizations.
Go emits keyUsage as critical; zlint enforces.
LE does not operate a TLS-clientAuth-only dedicated root; not applicable.
Informational. Not applicable to LE -- all LE cross-certs include serverAuth.
Ceremony does not set template.OCSPServer for cross-certs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286-296), so no OCSP URL is emitted.
Random 128-bit serials are non-sequential by construction (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225-229).
Only id-kp-serverAuth (and pre-2026-06-15 id-kp-clientAuth) can be emitted (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280); both apply to the public Internet.
After 2025-06-15 for Applicant PKI hierarchies: ceremony's default 'server' yields serverAuth-only (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:254-258).
LE does not operate S/MIME roots.
16 random bytes interpreted as positive big.Int fit in <=128 bits, well below 2^159 (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225-229,287). Zlint also covers DER length.
Only standard internet-PKI extensions are emitted (cmd/ceremony/cert.go fields).
Same as 4.7.3 -- anyPolicy is unreachable in ceremony (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:158-161).
Curve25519/Curve448 are not *ecdsa.PublicKey types and are rejected by goodkey (goodkey/good_key.go:165-171, 270-283 only permits NIST P-256/384/521).
goodkey requires exponent == 65537 (goodkey/good_key.go:308-310), which satisfies the BR floor of odd integer >=3.
Cross-key-reuse: not enforced in Boulder code; operational invariant for LE.
CRLs are signed with the issuer's HSM key using the same AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78 limits ceremony) or the online issuer's configured sigalg (issuance/issuer.go).
Serial is 16 random bytes from the HSM RNG via hsmRandReader (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225-229), then big.Int.SetBytes (so non-zero with overwhelming probability); zlint also flags zero serials.
Go stdlib emits canonical AlgorithmIdentifier encodings (explicit NULL for PKCS1, absent params for ECDSA); zlint cabf_br lints enforce.

ISRG Root X1/X2 are single-purpose TLS-serverAuth roots, not 'TLS (generic)' that combines server+client. The older DST cross of ISRG Root X1 is from a generic root but is now expired; for the active LE hierarchy this rule does not apply.
Same as 4.7.9 -- no qualifiers emitted at all.

Boulder ceremony does not support an 'unrestricted/anyEKU' shape: the only valid profile.EKUs values are 'server' or 'both' (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280), neither emits anyExtendedKeyUsage. LE never issues unrestricted cross-certs.
Ceremony does not emit emailProtection on crossCert; only serverAuth (+clientAuth pre-2026-06-15) (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280).

LE's CA-CRL (the CRL pointed to from a cross-cert's CRLDP) is emitted by ceremony with a critical IDP (onlyContainsCACerts) (cmd/ceremony/crl.go:39-43, crl/idp/idp.go:62-79). Apple §2.1.2 / CCADB §6.2 SHOULD-NOT discourage IDP when CCADB lists a single Full CRL URL. This is a SHOULD-NOT and may be flagged depending on how the CRL is disclosed in CCADB.

crl/idp/idp.go only encodes the three fields (distributionPoint, onlyContainsUserCerts, onlyContainsCACerts) -- never indirectCRL or onlyContainsAttributeCerts. lint_crl_has_idp.go:117-122 rejects any extra fields.
1024-bit RSA is rejected by goodRSABitLen (goodkey/good_key.go:333-346).
Hard 8-year cap enforced at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:318-323 (`>= 8*365*24*time.Hour`), with the corresponding lint at linter/lints/cpcps/lint_subordinate_ca_cert_validity_period_greater_than_8_years.go.
Informational about audit scope. LE's cross-certs always carry serverAuth (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280) so they are correctly in TLS-audit scope.

All LE cross-certs transit TLS: profile defaults to 'server' (serverAuth only) or 'both' (serverAuth+clientAuth, only allowed pre-2026-06-15) (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280). Cross-cert EKUs must match tbcs.ExtKeyUsage (cert.go:282-284 and main.go:723-730), ensuring serverAuth presence when crossing an issuing intermediate.
MD5 is not in AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78); only SHA-2 enumerated.

stringToKeyUsage permits 'Cert Sign' (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:184) but the profile YAML must include it. No code-level requirement that keyCertSign be asserted in a crossCert; relies on YAML correctness. Zlint enforces presence for CA certs.
Informational only. LE's emitted EKUs (serverAuth, clientAuth) are in Microsoft's recognized set.
signAndWriteCert calls cert.CheckSignatureFrom(issuer) at cmd/ceremony/main.go:524, aborting if the signature does not verify under the issuing CA's public key.
Ceremony reads NotBefore from the YAML profile (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:304-307); there is no programmatic check that NotBefore <= signing time. Enforced procedurally during the ceremony.
Go's PKIX ECDSA SPKI marshalling emits the canonical AlgorithmIdentifier bytes per curve; zlint enforces.
goodkey allows only NIST P-256/P-384/P-521 in namedCurve form (goodkey/good_key.go:270-283); Go emits namedCurve by default.
cmd/ceremony/main.go:691 sets template.AuthorityKeyId = issuer.SubjectKeyId; Go emits this as the [0] keyIdentifier inside AKI.
template.AuthorityKeyId set explicitly at cmd/ceremony/main.go:691 to issuer.SubjectKeyId, causing Go to emit the AKI extension.
profile.EKUs='both' (serverAuth+clientAuth) is allowed only when notBefore is before 2026-06-15 (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:269-277); otherwise default is 'server' (serverAuth-only).
template.BasicConstraintsValid=true and IsCA=true (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:288-289) cause Go to emit basicConstraints.
LE does not operate S/MIME roots.
LE does not operate clientAuth-purpose roots.
CRLDistributionPoints is mandatory for cross-certs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:148-151), satisfying Microsoft's CRL-or-OCSP requirement.

stringToKeyUsage map (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:181-185) only permits Digital Signature, CRL Sign, Cert Sign -- no way to set nonRepudiation/keyEncipherment/dataEncipherment/keyAgreement/encipherOnly/decipherOnly. Zlint also enforces.
LE does not operate S/MIME roots.

CRLNumber is set on template (cmd/ceremony/crl.go:19, issuance/crl.go:92); lint linter/lints/rfc/lint_crl_has_number.go enforces. Boulder online CRLs use monotonically increasing numbers from CA DB; ceremony CRLs derive number from YAML (validated non-zero at cmd/ceremony/main.go:427).
Go's x509.CreateCertificate never emits issuerUniqueID; the template type has no field for it. Zlint also covers this.
x509.CreateCertificate (called from signAndWriteCert in cmd/ceremony/main.go:508) emits v3 unconditionally; zlint enforces v3 for CA certs.

RawSubject byte-equality check at cmd/ceremony/main.go:707-709 between toBeCrossSigned and lintCert. The Subject is constructed from CN/O/C in the profile (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96), so the YAML must reproduce the exact existing DN; mismatch aborts.
Go's x509.CreateCertificate AKI emits only the keyIdentifier (no authorityCertIssuer); zlint enforces.

Ceremony reads the subject pubkey from a separate PEM file (cmd/ceremony/main.go:671) and does not compare it byte-for-byte against toBeCrossSigned.RawSubjectPublicKeyInfo. SPKI byte-identity is enforced operationally by feeding the same key file used to mint the original cert; an explicit code check would be a hardening improvement.
Profile inherits NotAfter from YAML; root-program lifecycle bounds are addressed in 2.5.5/2.5.6.
Same as 4.2.4 -- no authorityCertSerialNumber emitted by Go's AKI encoder.

x509.CreateRevocationList sets issuer to issuer.Subject (cmd/ceremony/crl.go:55, with issuer parameter); for online CRLs the issuer is issuance.Issuer.Cert.Subject (issuance/crl.go:119). Linter/lints/rfc/lint_crl_has_issuer_name.go checks DN presence.
MakeUserCertsExt (crl/idp/idp.go:35-60) sets only onlyContainsUserCerts; MakeCACertsExt sets only onlyContainsCACerts; neither sets both. lint_crl_has_idp.go:124-128 enforces.
goodkey allows only *rsa.PublicKey and *ecdsa.PublicKey (goodkey/good_key.go:165-171); EdDSA pubkeys are rejected.
Ceremony's certProfile has no field for policyQualifiers (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:19-21, 339-345); Go emits the policy with no qualifiers.
LE's cross-cert subject keys are RSA 2048 e=65537 or ECDSA P-384 in practice; goodkey would also accept RSA 3072/4096 and P-256/P-521, which LE does not currently use.
Procedural pre-condition: this profile is used only when the existing cert was BR-compliant at issuance. LE's existing certs are BR-compliant LE-issued certs; not encoded in source.
Exponent == 65537 (goodkey/good_key.go:308-310), so trivially != 1.
MaxPathLenZero is inherited from tbcs at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:332-334, which is correct for a cross-certified subordinate. MAY be present.
AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78) restricts to SHA-256/384/512 + RSA-PKCS1/ECDSA. Zlint enforces BR-permitted encodings.
At least one keyUsage bit must be set or ceremony fails (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:239-241); ku is then set on the template, which Go emits.
16 bytes (128 bits) come from the HSM CSPRNG via randReader from openSigner (cmd/ceremony/main.go:683 and cert.go:225-229).
Go's x509 PKIX RSA SPKI emits rsaEncryption with explicit NULL parameters; zlint cabf_br lints enforce this byte pattern.
Exactly one Reserved Policy OID (2.23.140.1.2.1) is always present on cross-certs by ceremony enforcement (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:158-161).
Go marks basicConstraints critical when IsCA=true; zlint covers this.

LE's existing roots and TLS subordinate CAs do not include organizationalUnitName (Subject() in cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96 omits OU), so cross-certs inherit that. Zlint also enforces no OU in CA certs under cabf_br.
AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78) limits to SHA-{256,384,512} with RSA-PKCS1 or ECDSA. PSS is not configured; Go emits canonical AlgorithmIdentifier encodings.
LE does not operate code-signing roots.
Go emits EKU non-critical; zlint enforces.
After 2026-06-15, ceremony forbids 'both' (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:269-277) so only [serverAuth] can be emitted.
Inherited via byte-identical Subject: zlint applied to LE's existing intermediates/roots already verified §7.1.4 attribute encoding at issuance time. LE's roots/intermediates conform to §7.1.4.
goodkey.GoodKey is invoked in loadPubKey (cmd/ceremony/main.go:555) and again post-issuance (main.go:89); it only allows RSA or ECDSA (goodkey/good_key.go:165-167).
LE does not operate email-trust roots.
Reserved DV policy OID 2.23.140.1.2.1 is the only allowed value (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:156-161).

Operational revocation obligation for any extant SHA-1 sub-CA cert; LE has no SHA-1 sub-CA cert in its currently-active hierarchy (its outstanding SHA-1 cross from DST is expired). No code in Boulder governs this.
Boulder ceremony does not allow SHA-1 (AllowedSigAlgs at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78), so the 2026-09-15 sunset is moot for LE.
Go's x509.CreateRevocationList emits AKI automatically; lint linter/lints/rfc/lint_crl_has_aki.go enforces presence and non-critical.
LE does not issue EV certs (DV only); the §6.3 EV policy-OID requirement does not apply.
All LE cross-certs are part of TLS hierarchies, so this branch is not exercised; ceremony defaults to 'server' EKU (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:254-258).
x509.CreateCertificate copies issuer.RawSubject into the new cert's Issuer field; the issuer cert is loaded from disk at cmd/ceremony/main.go:675.
Ceremony validate forces RevocationReason on every revoked entry (cmd/ceremony/main.go:436-438); lint linter/lints/cabf_br/lint_crl_acceptable_reason_codes.go restricts to 1/3/4/5/9.

Ceremony CA-CRL is the single-Full-CRL form. The sharded shard CRLs in issuance/crl.go:103-105 emit a critical IDP with URL matching the CRLDP. The CCADB JSON-array case applies to sharded leaf CRLs, where the per-shard IDP URL matches the per-shard CCADB-disclosed URL.

Go's AIA emitter only supports id-ad-ocsp (template.OCSPServer) and id-ad-caIssuers (template.IssuingCertificateURL); ceremony only sets caIssuers (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:216-218), no other accessMethods possible.
Ceremony CRL caps nextUpdate-thisUpdate at 365 days (cmd/ceremony/crl.go:33-37); linter/lints/cabf_br/lint_crl_validity_period.go enforces broadly.
Ceremony never emits anyExtendedKeyUsage on crossCert (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280); only serverAuth or serverAuth+clientAuth.
verifyProfile requires IssuerURL (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:152-154); ceremony sets template.IssuingCertificateURL (cert.go:216-218), so AIA (caIssuers only) is emitted.
Go's x509 stdlib produces valid DER; zlint and pkimetal (linter/lints/rfc/lint_cert_via_pkimetal.go) verify.
Go emits SKI as non-critical.
LE does not operate email-trust roots.
Subjective; LE's emitted extensions match standard semantics.
Go emits certificatePolicies non-critical; zlint enforces.
Ceremony does not validate the scheme of profile.CRLURL; relies on YAML correctness. Zlint cabf_br lints cover http scheme check.
LE does not operate timestamping roots.
LE-issued cross-certs do not carry nameConstraints (no support in ceremony certProfile); rule trivially satisfied.
Exactly one URL in CRLDistributionPoints (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:212-214 wraps the single profile.CRLURL).
LE does not issue delegated-OCSP-signing cross-certs.
Informational about audit scope; Boulder/ceremony never emits anyExtendedKeyUsage or empty EKU on a cross-cert (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:243-280 always sets at least one EKU on non-root).
Profile-driven NotAfter (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:308-311); no explicit check that NotAfter >= signing time. Procedural during ceremony.
Boulder/ceremony does not validate URL liveness pre-issuance. LE's CRL service is operated by Boulder's crl-updater and ceremony-issued CA-CRLs; ensuring CRL availability is operational.
verifyProfile requires exactly one policy with OID 2.23.140.1.2.1 (DV) for crossCert (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:148-161); policies are emitted via cert.Policies (cert.go:339-345).
notes.intermediates::intermediate-subordinate-: Chrome's 3-year SHOULD; LE intermediates have historically been ~5 years. Not enforced by ceremony or lints; LE policy choice.
Only 'rsa' and 'ecdsa' (with P-256/P-384/P-521) allowed (cmd/ceremony/main.go:103-122). Curve25519/Curve448 not supported.
stringToKeyUsage map (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:181-185) restricts to exactly {Digital Signature, Cert Sign, CRL Sign}, no other bits possible.
After signing, ceremony tool calls cert.CheckSignatureFrom(issuer) (cmd/ceremony/main.go:524).
Applies only to Technically Constrained TLS Sub-CA; not LE's profile.
Ceremony's intermediateCeremony sets template.AuthorityKeyId = issuer.SubjectKeyId (cmd/ceremony/main.go:635), same for crossCertCeremony (main.go:691). Go x509 marshals it as the AKI extension.
Go x509.CreateRevocationList emits version 1 (v2 CRL). zlint validates.
Go x509 marks SKI non-critical.
Only 2048 or 4096 allowed (cmd/ceremony/main.go:116-118); 1024 rejected. goodkey also requires ≥2048.
Go x509.CreateCertificate never emits UniqueIdentifier fields; cert template at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286 sets no SubjectUniqueId.

AllowedSigAlgs map (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78) restricts to RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 SHA-256/384/512 and ECDSA SHA-256/384/512. RSASSA-PSS isn't a ceremony option but Go's x509 marshaller would emit the prescribed encoding if used.
LE intermediates set 'Digital Signature' for OCSP delegation. Operationally configured via YAML.

'server' produces only serverAuth; 'both' produces serverAuth+clientAuth (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280). Both meet Chrome's pre-2026-06-15 rule. 'both' is rejected if notBefore is on/after 2026-06-15 (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:273-277).
cert.BasicConstraintsValid=true and IsCA=true (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286-296). Go x509 always emits the extension when BasicConstraintsValid.

Boulder's linter.makeLintCert (linter/linter.go:249) explicitly verifies bytes.Equal(issuer.RawSubject, lintCert.RawIssuer); Go x509.CreateCertificate copies issuer.RawSubject byte-for-byte into the new cert.
generateSKID uses RFC 7093 method 1 (truncated SHA-256 of SPK BIT STRING, cmd/ceremony/cert.go:187-202), a valid RFC 5280 §4.2.1.2 method. Unique per public key.
LE intermediates do not have nameConstraints; this requirement applies only when present.
Serial is 16 bytes from the HSM RNG via newRandReader (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225-229, ecdsa.go uses session-backed reader). 128 bits >> 64 bits required.
Same as 4-6.13: ceremony produces only serverAuth or serverAuth+clientAuth (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280).
LE intermediates do not have nameConstraints.
LE intermediates do not have nameConstraints.
Subject DN built from a minimal set of fields (C, O, CN) (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96). zlint applies the full BR 7.1.4 suite to the lint cert.
pkix.Name struct used in ceremony does not populate OrganizationalUnit (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96) — only Country, Organization, CommonName.
AllowedSigAlgs only permits SHA-256/384/512 variants (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78); MD5 and other algorithms rejected.
AllowedSigAlgs map forbids SHA-1 signature algorithms entirely (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78); the exception is moot.
LE does not have any SHA-1 intermediates outstanding; AllowedSigAlgs forbids SHA-1 issuance. Out-of-scope as descriptive/historical.
Ceremony cert template only populates IssuingCertificateURL (caIssuers); no other accessMethod can be added via ceremony YAML. OCSPServer field is not exposed via certProfile.
policyInfoConfig (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:19-21) only carries an OID — no qualifier fields exist in the ceremony config schema, so qualifiers are never emitted.
Go x509 marks basicConstraints critical when IsCA=true. zlint e_basic_constraints_not_critical enforces.

Serial generated as 16 random bytes from HSM-backed CSPRNG (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225-229). big.Int.SetBytes interprets unsigned, so > 0 unless all-zero (1/2^128 chance). Also enforced by zlint e_invalid_certificate_version / e_serial_number_signed.
Go's x509 marshaller emits the required namedCurve OID encoding for each curve. zlint enforces.
Out of scope for LE: LE issues only TLS Sub-CAs, not Non-TLS Sub-CAs (BR 7.1.2.3).
Go's x509.AuthorityKeyId field only emits keyIdentifier; no authorityCertIssuer is set.
Applies only to Technically Constrained TLS Sub-CA; not LE's profile.
cert.IsCA=true (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:289).
verifyProfile requires IssuerURL for intermediateCert/crossCert (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:152-154). makeTemplate populates cert.IssuingCertificateURL (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:215-218, 292).
Out of scope for LE: LE issues only TLS Sub-CAs, not Non-TLS Sub-CAs (BR 7.1.2.3).
goodkey.GoodKey is called on the public key (cmd/ceremony/main.go:58, 89, 460); for ECDSA it runs partial public-key validation (goodkey/good_key.go:186-282).
Go's x509 marshaller only emits namedCurve form. PKCS#11 CKA_EC_PARAMS is set to the DER-encoded curve OID (cmd/ceremony/ecdsa.go:22-26, 43).

Cross-cert ceremony at cmd/ceremony/main.go:723-730 enforces lintCert EKUs match toBeCrossSigned cert's EKUs (or are exactly [serverAuth] / [serverAuth,clientAuth] when cross-signing a root). This addresses the Chrome public-key-reuse concern. Mostly procedural.
Go x509.CreateCertificate emits identical outer signatureAlgorithm and inner tbsCertificate.signature. zlint e_signature_algorithm_match enforces.
All extensions emitted are public Internet PKI standard extensions; no proprietary extensions used.
rsaExp is hardcoded to 65537 (cmd/ceremony/rsa.go:14-16, used in CKA_PUBLIC_EXPONENT). goodkey rejects anything else (goodkey/good_key.go:308-310).
verifyProfile (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:156-160) rejects any OID other than 2.23.140.1.2.1; anyPolicy cannot be configured.
Go's x509.CreateCertificate always emits v3 certificates when extensions are present (cmd/ceremony/main.go:508). Ceremony cert templates always include extensions (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286).

MakeUserCertsExt sets only OnlyContainsUserCerts (crl/idp/idp.go:35-60); MakeCACertsExt sets only OnlyContainsCACerts (crl/idp/idp.go:64-79). linter/lints/cpcps/lint_crl_has_idp.go:124-128 checks they aren't both true.
LE roots are not S/MIME-purpose.

NotBefore is set from the YAML profile (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:304). Operationally set at/before signing; zlint e_subca_no_dn_or_certPol_check etc. and zlint cert linting catches future-dated. Some operational care needed.
verifyProfile requires IssuerURL (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:152-154); LE intermediate AIA carries caIssuers. OCSP URL field is not configurable (no profile.OCSPURL).
NotAfter is set from YAML; ceremony enforces notAfter ≥ notBefore via validity calculation (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:312). Past-dated notAfter would fail subsequent lints.

Across a CA's lifetime, the subject DN must byte-match earlier issued certs. Boulder lints (linter/linter.go:249) compare lint cert's RawIssuer to the issuer's RawSubject. For intermediates issued from a long-lived root, operationally the YAML profile must produce a consistent encoding (same Go version).
Only 'none', 'server', 'both' EKU modes accepted (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280); anyExtendedKeyUsage is not configurable.
AllowedSigAlgs forbids SHA-1 entirely (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78); Mozilla's narrow exception is moot.
stringToKeyUsage map (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:181-185) only contains Digital Signature, CRL Sign, Cert Sign — no other bits can be set.
verifyProfile requires CRLURL for intermediates (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:149-151); CRLDistributionPoints is always present.
Go x509 marks keyUsage critical by default. zlint enforces.
Go x509 marshaller emits the prescribed byte encodings; zlint validates.
Boulder uses Go's IssuingCertificateURL field which emits id-ad-caIssuers. URL scheme is operationally http (per LE ceremony YAML), not validated in code.
SignatureAlgorithm restricted to {SHA256/384/512WithRSA, ECDSAWithSHA256/384/512} (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78). zlint enforces exact AlgorithmIdentifier byte encoding (e_signature_algorithm_*).
pkix.Name is encoded as RDNSequence with one ATV per RDN by Go's x509 (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96 uses single-valued slices). zlint enforces.
Random 128-bit serials make collisions infeasible; ceremony tool only generates one cert per run. Uniqueness across the issuing CA's lifetime is operationally assured by the random serial.
Not explicitly checked in ceremony tool; would require comparing intermediate.NotAfter to issuer.NotAfter. Operationally controlled.
Both 'server' and 'both' EKU modes include x509.ExtKeyUsageServerAuth (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:254-280).

Go encodes the URI as uniformResourceIdentifier GeneralName. The http scheme is operationally enforced by the ceremony YAML; not validated in code. zlint e_old_root_ca_rsa_mod_less_than_2048_bits etc. catch many BR issues.
Applies only to Technically Constrained TLS Sub-CA; not LE's profile.
template.AuthorityKeyId = issuer.SubjectKeyId (cmd/ceremony/main.go:635, 691).
cert.SubjectKeyId is always set (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:220, 295); generateSKID computes leftmost 160 bits of SHA-256 of subjectPublicKey BIT STRING (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:187-202).
keyGenConfig restricts Type to 'rsa' or 'ecdsa' (cmd/ceremony/main.go:113-115). goodkey policy in ceremony rejects other key types (cmd/ceremony/main.go:36-40).
Go's x509.CreateCertificate (cmd/ceremony/main.go:508) emits identical AlgorithmIdentifier bytes in tbsCertificate.signature and outer signatureAlgorithm. zlint e_signature_algorithm_match enforces.
RSAModLength restricted to {2048, 4096} (cmd/ceremony/main.go:116-118). goodkey policy further constrains via goodKeyRSA (goodkey/good_key.go:333-345).
Applies to Applicant PKI (operator-controlled hierarchies under a different CA); LE operates its own root program-enrolled hierarchies.
Ceremony produces serverAuth or serverAuth+clientAuth (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280); both permitted by Mozilla §7.5.1.
Go x509 emits CRLDistributionPoints with fullName only, no reasons, no cRLIssuer.
All LE-issued intermediates use C=US, O=Let's Encrypt, CN=<short name> via ceremony YAML (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96). This is operational/policy.
Ceremony cert template (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286-347) only sets the standard fields; no ExtraExtensions are added for intermediates.
Only standard extensions are emitted (AKI, BC, KU, SKI, EKU, CP, CDP, AIA); no misleading semantics.
Out of scope for LE: LE issues only TLS Sub-CAs, not Non-TLS Sub-CAs (BR 7.1.2.3).
LE intermediates do not have nameConstraints.
Only 'none', 'server', 'both' EKU modes accepted (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280); codeSigning is not configurable.
Ceremony tool only generates plain rsaEncryption keys via PKCS#11 CKM_RSA_PKCS_KEY_PAIR_GEN (cmd/ceremony/rsa.go:28). Never id-RSASSA-PSS.
Cross-cert ceremony at cmd/ceremony/main.go:716-730 ensures EKU consistency for cross-signs. Same-org check at main.go:736-739 prevents cross-signing different operators' certs.
verifyProfile requires CRLURL for intermediateCert/crossCert (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:149-151); makeTemplate populates cert.CRLDistributionPoints (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:211-214, 291).
LE intermediates do not have nameConstraints.
Ceremony produces only id-kp-serverAuth (default) or serverAuth+clientAuth ('both') (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280).
Operational: LE must keep the CRL service running. Boulder's crl-updater (crl/updater/updater.go) generates CRLs continuously.
Only one policy is configurable (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:158); it's the first (and only) PolicyInformation.
Online CRLs add an IDP with the shard's URL (issuance/crl.go:103-108). linter/lints/cpcps/lint_crl_has_idp.go:101-150 verifies URL matches expectations.
Serial is 16 bytes (128 bits) (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225) so < 2^128 < 2^159. zlint e_serial_number_longer_than_159_bits enforces.
Go x509 marks AIA non-critical.
CRLURL field is a single URL (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:46-47); produces exactly one DistributionPoint.
intermediateCert and crossCert always get EKUs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:252-281); default 'server' adds id-kp-serverAuth.
LE intermediates do not have nameConstraints.
All recent LE intermediates are P-384 ECDSA or 2048-bit RSA with E=65537 per ceremony configs. RSAModLength=2048 and ECDSACurve=P-384 are LE's standard choices (cmd/ceremony/main.go:103-118).
Only 'none', 'server', 'both' EKU modes accepted (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280); precertificate-signing OID is not configurable.
Out of scope for LE: LE issues only TLS Sub-CAs, not Non-TLS Sub-CAs (BR 7.1.2.3).
Only 'none', 'server', 'both' EKU modes accepted (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280); timeStamping is not configurable.
policyInfoConfig schema (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:19-21) has no qualifier field; no qualifiers possible.
Only 'none', 'server', 'both' EKU modes accepted (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280); emailProtection is not configurable.
stringToKeyUsage map includes 'CRL Sign' (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:181-185). Operationally always set in LE ceremony YAML; not enforced as MUST in code.
profile.Country required (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:138-140); LE uses 'US'. zlint e_subject_country_not_iso enforces ISO format; Go pkix.Name encodes countryName as PrintableString by default.
KeyUsage is required (at least one bit) by verifyProfile and makeTemplate (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:231-241).
pkix.Name fields are single-valued (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:90-96).
Exponent is hardcoded 65537 (cmd/ceremony/rsa.go:14-16), enforced by goodkey (goodkey/good_key.go:308). Cannot be 1.
All extensions are emitted via Go's x509 standard DER encoding.
LE intermediates are not technically constrained; they use the unconstrained TLS Sub-CA profile (BR 7.1.2.6).
Only 'none', 'server', 'both' EKU modes accepted (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280); OCSPSigning is not configurable.
Go x509 marks cRLDistributionPoints non-critical.

Ceremony YAML allows mixing curves and hashes; not directly enforced (e.g. P-384 with SHA-256 would be accepted by ceremony). zlint e_ecdsa_curve_size_signature_size_mismatch should catch. Operationally LE pairs correctly.
Applies only to Technically Constrained TLS Sub-CA; not LE's profile.
LE intermediates do not carry the SCT List extension; SCTs are only on subscriber certs.
MaxPathLenZero=true is set for intermediateCert (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:329-331), or matches existing cert for crossCert.
LE intermediates do not have nameConstraints.
Code path at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:273-277 rejects 'both' for notBefore >= 2026-06-15, leaving only 'server' (id-kp-serverAuth only).
Issuer signing algorithm uses the same AllowedSigAlgs map (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78); zlint enforces signature AlgorithmIdentifier on CRLs.
LE uses partitioned (sharded) CRLs disclosed as a JSON Array, not a single Full CRL URL.
Out of scope for LE: LE issues only TLS Sub-CAs, not Non-TLS Sub-CAs (BR 7.1.2.3).
LE roots are not Client-Authentication-purpose.
LE roots are not Timestamping-purpose.

Go's encoding/asn1 emits DER. Post-issuance, ceremony tool re-parses the cert with zlintx509 (cmd/ceremony/main.go:74-78) and runs the full lint suite which includes DER strictness checks (pkimetal etc.).

issuance/crl.go:116-121 passes i.Cert.Certificate (the issuer cert) to x509.CreateRevocationList, which copies its subject as the CRL issuer. zlint and linter/lints/rfc/lint_crl_has_issuer_name.go check.
verifyProfile (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:156-160) requires exactly one policy OID and that OID is 2.23.140.1.2.1 (CABF BR DV).
Only 'rsa' and 'ecdsa' allowed (cmd/ceremony/main.go:113-115); EdDSA is not supported.
cert.MaxPathLenZero=true for intermediateCert (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:329-331).
Go pkix.Name encoding produces C, O, CN in the order required by BR 7.1.4.2. zlint validates ordering.
Go's x509 marshaller emits the canonical RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 AlgorithmIdentifier with explicit NULL parameters. zlint e_signature_algorithm_*_params_present enforces.
'both' mode adds clientAuth (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:259-277) — note: only allowed for notBefore before 2026-06-15.
Go x509.CreateCertificate marks AKI non-critical by default. zlint e_ext_authority_key_identifier_critical enforces.
Each Go pkix.Name attribute becomes its own RDN with one AttributeTypeAndValue; zlint enforces multi-valued RDN restrictions (e_subject_dn_has_multi_value_rdn).
LE intermediates always include id-kp-serverAuth in EKU (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:254-280), so they're intentionally in-scope for Mozilla TLS issuance.
Go x509.CreateCertificate never emits UniqueIdentifier fields (cmd/ceremony/main.go:508 — no IssuerUniqueId field set on cert template at cmd/ceremony/cert.go:286).
Only 'server' (id-kp-serverAuth) or 'both' (serverAuth + clientAuth) are possible (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:253-280).
cmd/ceremony/cert.go:318-322 rejects validity >= 8*365 days for intermediateCert/crossCert. Also enforced by linter/lints/cpcps/lint_subordinate_ca_cert_validity_period_greater_than_8_years.go.
Out-of-scope operational requirement: the YAML CRL URL must match CCADB disclosure. Not enforced in Boulder code.
Applies only to Technically Constrained TLS Sub-CA; not LE's profile.
LE intermediates do not have nameConstraints.
allowedCurves = {P-256, P-384, P-521} (cmd/ceremony/main.go:103-107); Go marshals in namedCurve form.
verifyProfile (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:156-160) rejects anyPolicy; only the BR domain-validated OID is allowed. LE doesn't use the affiliate provision.
verifyProfile (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:148-161) requires exactly one policy for intermediate and crossCert. cert.Policies is populated from profile.Policies (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:339-345).
Go's x509.AuthorityKeyId field only emits keyIdentifier; no authorityCertSerialNumber is set.
LE intermediates do not have nameConstraints.
Out of scope for LE: LE issues only TLS Sub-CAs, not Non-TLS Sub-CAs (BR 7.1.2.3).
LE intermediates do not carry the SCT List extension.
LE does not issue delegated OCSP responder certificates as intermediates; OCSP is signed directly by intermediates (digitalSignature KU).

For subscriber-cert CRLs (online), issuance/crl.go:103-108 adds a critical IDP with only the shard URL. linter/lints/cpcps/lint_crl_has_idp.go enforces. For sub-CA CRLs (ceremony), MakeCACertsExt creates a critical IDP without distributionPoint (crl/idp/idp.go:64-79).
Ceremony reads notBefore from YAML; no automated check that it's within 1 day before signing time. Operationally controlled during key ceremony.
Applies only to Technically Constrained TLS Sub-CA (BR 7.1.2.5); LE intermediates use the unconstrained TLS Sub-CA profile (BR 7.1.2.6).
Go x509 marks EKU non-critical by default.
Ceremony tool does not configure RSASSA-PSS signing; AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78) only contains PKCS1-v1_5 variants for RSA.
LE intermediates do not include OCSP URL in their AIA (intermediate AIA only has caIssuers).
Go x509 marks certificatePolicies non-critical.
Applies only to Technically Constrained TLS Sub-CA; not LE's profile.
Out of scope for LE: LE issues only TLS Sub-CAs, not Non-TLS Sub-CAs (BR 7.1.2.3).
Only 2048 or 4096 are configurable (cmd/ceremony/main.go:116-118), both divisible by 8.
AllowedSigAlgs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:71-78) excludes SHA-1 entirely.
LE roots/intermediates do not have the email trust bit; LE only issues TLS certs.
Boulder doesn't validate the URL scheme/host; operationally LE configures the correct CRL service URL in the ceremony YAML.
Serial is fully random 16 bytes from CSPRNG (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:225-229), not sequential.
profile.Organization required (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:135-137); LE uses 'Let's Encrypt' (14 chars). Go encodes as PrintableString/UTF8String per content. zlint enforces length and encoding.
Go x509.CreateRevocationList automatically adds the AKI extension. linter/lints/rfc/lint_crl_has_aki.go enforces presence.

crl/idp/idp.go's issuingDistributionPoint struct does not include indirectCRL or onlyContainsAttributeCerts fields, so they're never asserted. linter/lints/cpcps/lint_crl_has_idp.go verifies no extra fields.

stringToKeyUsage map (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:181-185) maps 'Cert Sign' to keyCertSign. Ceremony YAML must list it; operationally LE always does. Not enforced as a MUST in code; zlint e_ca_subject_field_empty / e_ca_no_cert_sign_or_crl_sign should catch.

All LE intermediates (issued after 2019) have EKU present via ceremony (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:252-281). Cross-cert exception is honored by the crossCert path which can preserve the toBeCrossSigned cert's EKUs (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:282-284, 723-730).
LE roots/intermediates do not have the email trust bit; LE only issues TLS certs.
CRL Number derived from thisUpdate.UnixNano() (crl/crl.go:17-23), monotonically increasing. linter/lints/rfc/lint_crl_has_number.go enforces presence. Number is well below 2^159.
rsaExp=65537 hardcoded (cmd/ceremony/rsa.go:14-16). goodkey checkSmallPrimes (goodkey/good_key.go:315-317, smallPrimeInts up to 752) enforces no factors < 752 and that modulus is odd.
Out of scope for LE: LE issues only TLS Sub-CAs, not Non-TLS Sub-CAs (BR 7.1.2.3).
Applies to Applicant PKI; LE is its own root operator.
Ceremony CRL signer rejects validity > 12 months (cmd/ceremony/crl.go:35-37). Online subscriber CRLs are < 10 days (issuance/crl.go:38-39 caps lifetime ≤ 10 days).
Informational requirement; LE-issued intermediates always have id-kp-serverAuth (optionally id-kp-clientAuth), both within Microsoft's enabled set.
Go's x509 marshaller emits the standard rsaEncryption AlgorithmIdentifier with explicit NULL params, producing the required 300d06092a864886f70d0101010500 byte sequence.
Go x509 marshaller emits ECDSA AlgorithmIdentifiers without a parameters field. zlint enforces.
verifyProfile hardcodes the required OID 2.23.140.1.2.1 (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:158).
profile.CommonName required (cmd/ceremony/cert.go:132-134); short LE intermediate names ('E5', 'R10', etc.) well under 64 chars. zlint enforces.
notes.leaves::tls-subscriber-leaf-certificate-: SCTs are obtained synchronously by ctpolicy.GetSCTs during issuance (ca/ca.go:368-381); precert is logged before the SCT is returned, so logging happens within milliseconds of issuance.
Same as 4.6.5; timeStamping never included.
Precertificates and final certificates share the same sigAlg (issuance/issuer.go:212-227 chooses per-issuer, used for both); SHA-1 is never selected.
Go copies issuer.SubjectKeyId into the keyIdentifier field. zlint lint_ext_authority_key_identifier_no_key_identifier enforces presence.
Same as 4.4.4. Go's crypto/x509 doesn't emit authorityCertSerialNumber, and zlint's AKI lints enforce.
Serials are produced by crypto/rand reading 17 random bytes per call (ca.go:480-493); not sequential.
Boulder only ever sets IssuingCertificateURL (caIssuers) in the template (issuance/cert.go:179). zlint lint_aia_must_contain_permitted_access_method enforces.
goodkey enforces E == 65537 (good_key.go:308-310), excluding 1. zlint lint_mp_exponent_cannot_be_one is also in the registry.
precert/corr.go Correspond verifies byte-equality of the first 7 TBS fields and the remaining extensions when poison and SCT-List are removed (corr.go:47-107).

Boulder uses a one-byte prefix in [0x01,0x7f] plus 17 random bytes (136 bits) = 18 bytes <= 2^144, well under 2^159 (ca.go:480-493). issuance/cert.go:159-161 also rejects encoded serials over 19 bytes.
Boulder only sets CN. zlint lint_invalid_subject_rdn_order enforces RDN ordering.
Applies to IV certs only; LE issues only DV.
issuance/issuer.go:212-227 never picks a SHA-1-based sigAlg; only SHA-256/384 are emitted. zlint lint_sub_cert_or_sub_ca_using_sha1 also enforces.
issuance/cert.go:179 always sets IssuingCertificateURL, which makes Go emit AIA with id-ad-caIssuers.
Go's crypto/x509.CreateCertificate writes both fields from the same SignatureAlgorithm value (issuance/issuer.go:212-227). zlint lint_tbs_signature_alg_matches_cert_signature_alg enforces.
Boulder's WFE2 exposes /acme/renewal-info/ (wfe2/wfe.go:69, 454) and implements RFC 9773 ARI (wfe.go:2141-2208).
Same as 1.8.1; Boulder never sets subjectUniqueID and zlint lint_cert_contains_unique_identifier catches presence.
Boulder does not set organizationIdentifier (only CN on subscriber certs).

Hard-coded ceiling in issuance/cert.go:80-82 rejects MaxValidityPeriod >= 100 days. zlint lint e_subscriber_cert_validity_period_greater_than_100_days (linter/lints/cpcps/lint_subscriber_cert_validity_greater_than_100_days.go) enforces this against the linting cert.

Boulder only ever asserts digitalSignature [+ keyEncipherment for RSA] (issuance/cert.go:320-329). zlint lint_sub_cert_key_usage_cert_sign_bit_set / lint_sub_cert_key_usage_crl_sign_bit_set / lint_ecdsa_ee_invalid_ku enforce.
Boulder does not include an OCSP URL in AIA on subscriber certs (no OCSPServer is set). Mozilla's rule therefore does not apply.
Serials are 136 random bits from crypto/rand following an 8-bit prefix (ca/ca.go:480-493), far exceeding the 64-bit CSPRNG minimum.
Boulder's hard cap is <89 days (ra/ra.go:335), which exceeds the 10-day window required for 2029-03-15+ issuance. Both the cap and operational config must be tightened by that date.

Boulder's DV profile only sets CN (issuance/cert.go:314-316) and never countryName. Profile OmitCommonName (cert.go:36) allows configuring to omit even CN. zlint cabf_br lints lint_cab_dv_conflicts_with_* would catch other attributes.
Go's crypto/x509 encodes from a single string URL as a fullName URI with no reasons or cRLIssuer.
Applies to IV certs only; LE issues only DV.

ca/ca.go calls sa.AddSerial before issuance (ca.go:274-282); the serials table has a unique constraint enforcing uniqueness across the CA. Precert and corresponding final cert intentionally share the same serial (RequestFromPrecert reuses serial from precert in issuance/cert.go:412-428).

This is a subscriber-operational requirement (automation test certs must be renewed at least every 30 days). Boulder enforces validity caps and supports short renewal via ACME, but does not enforce 'must be renewed within 30 days' itself; that is the disclosure operator's responsibility.
Boulder only emits ServerAuth and (optionally) ClientAuth.
For RSA, digitalSignature is always asserted (and keyEncipherment unless explicitly omitted) (issuance/cert.go:321-326).
Only CN may be set (issuance/cert.go:314-316). With a single attribute, the requirement is trivially satisfied. Go's crypto/x509 always encodes one ATV per RDN.

goodkey performs Debian-weak-key blocking via BlockedKeyCheckFunc (good_key.go:151-162, sa-backed), ROCA detection via rocacheck.IsWeak (good_key.go:320-322), and Fermat factorization with default 110 rounds (good_key.go:325-329, 381-425).
Allowed sizes 2048/3072/4096 are all divisible by 8 (good_key.go:333-346). zlint lint_mp_modulus_must_be_divisible_by_8 also enforces.
For RSA keys Boulder asserts keyEncipherment unless OmitKeyEncipherment is set (issuance/cert.go:36-39, 320-329).

issuance/cert.go:350-355 derives the shard from `serial mod crlShards`, then issuance/crl.go ensures the same shard appears in the CRL's IssuingDistributionPoint. By construction the cert points to the shard whose CRL includes it.
1024-bit RSA is rejected: only 2048/3072/4096 are accepted (goodkey/good_key.go:333-346).
jurisdictionCountry only appears in EV certs. LE issues only DV.
Go's crypto/x509 emits CRLDistributionPoints non-critical. zlint lint_ext_crl_distribution_marked_critical / lint_sub_cert_crl_distribution_points_marked_critical enforce.
Applies to OV certs only; LE issues only DV.
Only one caIssuers and zero OCSP entries are present, so uniqueness/ordering are trivially satisfied. zlint lint_aia_unique_locations enforces.
Boulder never sets organizationName on subscriber certs (issuance/cert.go only sets CN). zlint lint_subject_organization_name_max_length enforces lengths if any.
ra.go:335 caps ValidAuthzLifetime under 89 days, well under 200 days.

Boulder does not use RSASSA-PSS for signing subscriber certs; only RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 with SHA-256 is selected when the issuer is RSA (issuance/issuer.go:215-217). zlint lint_mp_pss_parameters_encoding_correct still verifies.
Boulder's goodkey only accepts *rsa.PublicKey (interpreted as rsaEncryption). id-RSASSA-PSS keys are not accepted. zlint lint_mp_rsassa-pss_in_spki enforces.
Boulder adds no additional policy OIDs (issuance/cert.go:167-183 sets only the DV OID).
issuance/cert.go NewProfile caps MaxValidityPeriod at <100 days (cert.go:80-82). Far below the 398-day cap.
DV only - Boulder doesn't include OV/EV subscriber-info attributes that would need 825-day reuse tracking.
Boulder never sets these attributes (only CN is set in issuance/cert.go:315). zlint contains length lints for each (rfc/lint_subject_*_max_length.go).
Applies to IV certs only; LE issues only DV.
Final cert path does not add ctPoisonExt (issuance/cert.go:335-348 only adds it when IncludeCTPoison is true). precert.Correspond catches double-poisoning.
issuance/cert.go:215-218 emits the SCT-List with no Critical flag (default false).
issuance/cert.go:355 sets exactly one CRLDistributionPoints URL.
Applies to EV certs only; LE issues only DV.
Final cert path always adds SCT-List extension when sctList is provided (issuance/cert.go:337-345).
Same as 4.12.2.15: SCT inclusion makes the final cert observable via CT logs immediately upon issuance.
Go's crypto/x509 automatically keeps SAN non-critical when subject is non-empty. zlint lint_ext_san_critical_with_subject_dn enforces.
Go's crypto/x509 emits the canonical byte encoding for each SignatureAlgorithm constant. zlint lint_signature_algorithm_not_supported and the cabf_br/mozilla algorithm encoding lints enforce.
Go's crypto/x509 automatically sets SAN critical iff subject is empty. zlint lint_ext_san_not_critical_without_subject enforces.

Boulder's policy/pa.go does not explicitly reject .in-addr.arpa / .ip6.arpa suffixes in dNSNames; iana.ExtractSuffix may accept arpa. zlint lint_subject_contains_reserved_arpa_ip / malformed_arpa_ip target subject CN rather than the SAN suffix rule effective 2026-03-15. Should add an explicit check before that effective date.
goodkey LetsEncryptCPS default (good_key.go:76-84) enables RSA 2048/3072/4096 and ECDSA P-256/P-384 only (note: not P-521 by default).
ctPoisonExt is hardcoded with Critical: true (issuance/cert.go:188-193).

Boulder uses csr.CNFromCSR (csr/csr.go:102-126) which returns either '' (omitted) or a CN that originates from a SAN (or the CSR's own CN if it matches). issuance/cert.go:314 only sets the CN if non-empty. ProfileConfig OmitCommonName at cert.go:36 supports omitting CN entirely. zlint lint_subject_common_name_not_exactly_from_san enforces correspondence.

csr.CNFromCSR (csr/csr.go:102-126) only allows a CN that is shortened from a SAN. For IP CNs it returns empty string (csr.go:108-111). csrlib.VerifyCSR rejects CSRs whose CN is an IP literal (csr.go:78-80). zlint lint_subject_common_name_not_exactly_from_san backs this up.
Boulder's hard-coded MaxValidityPeriod cap of <100 days (issuance/cert.go:80-82) is below the 200-day cap for the 2026-03-15 window.

ca/ca.go:368-381 always calls GetSCTs and inserts the returned SCTs into the final cert via issuance.RequestFromPrecert. ctpolicy.GetSCTs (ctpolicy.go:99-105) requires SCTs from two distinct operators. The linter's chrome lint e_scts_from_same_operator additionally enforces two-operator policy with at least one RFC6962-compliant log on every lint cert.

Boulder's 100-day hard-coded ceiling (issuance/cert.go:80-82) exceeds the 47-day cap that will be required on 2029-03-15. Operational config must be tightened (and the hard-coded ceiling in cert.go relaxed downward) before that date.
ca/ca.go generateSerialNumber() builds a 137-bit positive integer with the leading prefix byte 0x01-0x7f (ca.go:147-149,480-493). zlint lint_serial_number_not_positive also enforces.
Boulder always sets CRLDistributionPoints (issuance/cert.go:350-355) for every subscriber cert, regardless of OCSP-in-AIA presence.
csr/csr.go:18 enforces maxCNLength = 64. zlint lint_subject_common_name_max_length backs this up. Go's x509 encodes CN as PrintableString or UTF8String automatically.

Boulder uses the same Issuer (and therefore the same i.sigAlg, issuance/issuer.go:212-227) for both precert and final cert; tbsCertIsDeterministic (ca/ca.go:518-579) verifies TBS byte equality including the signature AlgorithmIdentifier.

ca/ca.go generateSKID computes the truncated SHA-256 of the BIT STRING of the SPKI (RFC 7093 method 1) (ca.go:495-516). The hash makes it deterministic per key, satisfying uniqueness for distinct keys.
issuance/cert.go:167-183 always sets Policies to [domainValidatedOID].
issuance/cert.go:350-355 sets the issuing CA's CRL URL (composed from issuer.crlURLBase + shard + .crl, issuance/crl.go:77-79).
Boulder always sets IssuingCertificateURL (issuance/cert.go:179), so caIssuers is always present.
For ECDSA, Boulder sets only digitalSignature (issuance/cert.go:327-329).
For ECDSA keys Boulder asserts only digitalSignature (issuance/cert.go:327-329).
issuance.RequestFromPrecert copies precert.SerialNumber.Bytes() (issuance/cert.go:419); precert.Correspond verifies (corr.go:47-51).
Boulder only accepts CSR-supplied keys (ca/ca.go:286-296 takes csr.PublicKey). It never generates subscriber keys.
Same as 4.6.5; anyExtendedKeyUsage never included. zlint lint_sub_cert_eku_check enforces.

Boulder only ever sets template.Subject.CommonName (issuance/cert.go:315) and no other subject attributes. zlint runs a broad suite of subject-encoding lints from cabf_br and rfc against the linting cert.
Applies to OV certs only; LE issues only DV.

goodkey/good_key.go goodCurve restricts to P-256/P-384/P-521 (good_key.go:271-284). Go's x509 emits namedCurve form. LetsEncryptCPS default omits P-521 (good_key.go:76-84) so production only allows P-256/P-384.
issuance/cert.go:320-329 always asserts digitalSignature.
Go's crypto/x509 emits SKID non-critical when included. zlint lint_ext_subject_key_identifier_critical enforces.
Boulder asserts only 2.23.140.1.2.1 (DV), one of the §7.1.6.1 TLS-server-auth reserved OIDs (issuance/cert.go:167-174).
Boulder never sets NameConstraints on subscriber templates. zlint lint_ext_name_constraints_not_in_ca catches NC outside CA certs.
Applies to OV certs only; LE issues only DV.
goodkey allowedKeys only accepts modulus bit lengths of exactly 2048, 3072, or 4096 (good_key.go:333-346).
issuance/cert.go:180 sets BasicConstraintsValid: true, causing Go to emit a BasicConstraints extension with cA=false.
issuance/cert.go:335-336 adds ctPoisonExt when IncludeCTPoison is true (ca.go:295 always sets IncludeCTPoison: true for the precert request).

x509.CreateCertificate writes the rsaEncryption AlgorithmIdentifier deterministically with NULL parameters. zlint lint_subject_public_key_info_improper_algorithm_object_identifier_encoding and lint_spki_rsa_encryption_parameter_not_null verify this.

Boulder sets only DNSNames/IPAddresses (issuance/cert.go:317-318); other GeneralName types are never populated. csr.VerifyCSR rejects URI or email entries in CSRs (csr/csr.go:66-71). zlint lint_ext_san_*_present enforce.
Precert path sets IncludeCTPoison without sctList (issuance/cert.go:335-348). zlint lint_precert_with_sct_list enforces.
goodkey/good_key.go:308-310 enforces E == 65537, which is odd and >= 3.
issuance/cert.go:300-309 always sets ExtKeyUsage to at least ServerAuth (+ optionally ClientAuth). zlint lint_sub_cert_eku_missing enforces.
The CRL URL is operational; Boulder serves CRLs from this URL via the crl-updater / crl-storer subsystems, so it is operational. Code at issuance/issuer.go:236-243 requires a configured URL.
Boulder always populates IssuingCertificateURL (issuance/cert.go:179), producing one caIssuers AccessDescription.

Boulder selects sigAlg from a fixed allowlist in issuance/issuer.go:212-227: SHA256WithRSA for RSA, ECDSAWithSHA256 for P-256, ECDSAWithSHA384 for P-384. All have CABF-prescribed byte encodings. zlint lint_signature_algorithm_not_supported enforces the BR set.
Boulder does not add any policyQualifiers (issuance/cert.go:167-183 only sets the policy OID with no qualifiers).
Boulder never sets MaxPathLen on subscriber templates. zlint lint_path_len_constraint_improperly_included enforces.
Definitional rule. Boulder does not currently issue short-lived certs (always includes CRLDP at issuance/cert.go:355), so this branch does not apply.
Boulder only sets at most one CN attribute (issuance/cert.go:315) and no others. zlint lint_duplicate_subject_attribs / lint_extra_subject_common_names enforce this.
Boulder loads only issuing CAs as Issuer (issuance/issuer.go) and signs the precert directly with the Issuing CA's key (cert.go:394). No Precertificate Signing CA is used.
Boulder never asserts keyAgreement/nonRepudiation/encipherOnly/decipherOnly (issuance/cert.go:320-329).
goodkey/good_key.go:174-260 implements NIST SP 800-56A Sections 5.6.2.3.2 Steps 1-4 (point-at-infinity, range, on-curve, order) for ECDSA public keys.
Applies to OV certs only; LE issues only DV.
issuance/cert.go only adds SAN, AIA, AKI, CertificatePolicies, EKU, KeyUsage, BasicConstraints, optionally SKID, CRLDP, and the poison-or-SCT extension. No other extensions are added.

Wildcard names are issued only against DNS-01 / dns-account-01 / dns-persist-01 authorizations (policy/pa.go:614-623), in line with BR 3.2.2.6. policy/pa.go ValidDomain enforces wildcard label rules (pa.go:314-344).
All extensions are produced by Go's crypto/x509 (DER-encoded). zlint lint_cert_ext_invalid_der enforces.

ra/ra.go ValidationProfile.ValidAuthzLifetime is capped at <89 days (ra.go:335). Each identifier is bound to an authz that must be Valid and unexpired at finalization. This is well within the 398-day cap for pre-2026 issuance.
Boulder sets IssuingCertificateURL from IssuerConfig (issuance/issuer.go:232-233, cert.go:179) and never sets OCSP. The CP/CPS no longer guarantees an OCSP URL.
Go's crypto/x509 emits certificatePolicies non-critical. zlint lint_sub_cert_certificate_policies_marked_critical enforces.
Applies to IV certs only; LE issues only DV.
crypto/x509.CreateCertificate emits tbs.signature == outer signatureAlgorithm by construction; zlint's lint_tbs_signature_alg_matches_cert_signature_alg also enforces.
issuance/cert.go:180 sets BasicConstraintsValid: true; Go emits it critical with cA=false.

policy/pa.go validNonWildcardDomain rejects underscores, invalid chars, internal names (no public suffix), bad labels, malformed IDN (pa.go:222-308). LDH and P-Label rules are enforced (pa.go:257-296). iana.ExtractSuffix requires an ICANN TLD (pa.go:299-303).

policy/pa.go ValidIP calls iana.IsReservedAddr (pa.go:368), which checks the embedded IANA special-purpose registry (iana/ip.go:44-81, 139). zlint lint_ext_san_contains_reserved_ip is also in the registry.

Go's crypto/x509.CreateCertificate always emits v3 (version=2) when extensions are present, which Boulder always sets (Policies, SAN, etc.) in issuance/cert.go. zlint's lint_invalid_certificate_version also enforces this.

issuance/issuer.go:238-243 requires issuerURL with HTTP scheme. Boulder does not set OCSPServer at all on subscriber certs (no OCSP issuance). zlint lint_aia_must_contain_permitted_access_method / lint_aia_ca_issuers_must_have_http_only / lint_aia_ocsp_must_have_http_only enforce.
Go's crypto/x509 marks SAN critical iff Subject is empty. When OmitCommonName is set (issuance/cert.go:36-37) or CSR has no usable CN, Subject is empty and SAN becomes critical automatically.
Boulder's MaxValidityPeriod cap of <100 days (issuance/cert.go:80) is at/below the 100-day cap for the 2027-03-15 window. Note: the cap uses strict less-than so 100 days exactly is rejected.
issuance/cert.go:300-309 always includes ExtKeyUsageServerAuth.
Boulder always asserts digitalSignature for both RSA and ECDSA (issuance/cert.go:320-329).
Same enforcement as 1.6.2.15. zlint's DV-conflict lints (lint_cab_dv_conflicts_with_locality/org/postal/province/street and lint_cab_dv_subject_invalid_values) cover this.
ca/ca.go:368-381 fetches SCTs for the specific precert DER and feeds them straight into the final cert. publisher and ctpolicy ensure PreCert log entry type is used.
ProfileConfig.OmitClientAuth (issuance/cert.go:40-42, 304-308) supports removing ClientAuth ahead of 2027-03-15. Operationally Boulder can issue with only ServerAuth.
goodkey/good_key.go:308-310 enforces E == 65537, which is exactly 2^16+1, within the SHOULD range.
goodkey only allows RSA and ECDSA (good_key.go:143-150). Ed25519/Ed448 keys are rejected before issuance.
